Author: Daniel Yefet
Title: GitHub Copilot is a Game-Changer
Text:
GitHub Copilot is a Game-Changer
It’s time to jump on the bandwagon!
If you’re anything like me, you might have dismissed some of the new AI tooling as gimmicky. But since the recent announcement that GitHub Copilot is now free, I thought I’d give it a proper go — and boy am I glad I did! It’s so much more than glorified autocomplete; it’s become an integral part of my workflow and I’m not looking back anytime soon.
Getting the Most from Personal Projects
For me, personal projects are an opportunity to get creative, innovative, and fully immerse myself in learning. These experiences often accelerate my growth even more than my day job, ultimately improving my skills and enabling me to bring fresh insights back to work — it’s a satisfying, self-sustaining cycle!
However, what I seriously do not like is getting bogged down with frustrating or menial tasks, or things that I normally rely on other (smarter) people for. My time is precious, and I want to focus on learning things that truly matter to me.
This is where Copilot has had a massive impact for me. It’s like working with a dream team of overachievers who can write code, craft content, and enthusiastically answer every single one of my endless questions.
Content Creation
One of the first surprising things for me was how Copilot just intuitively knew what I wanted to convey to users.
Alongside a few others, I’m developing Goalite to help people fulfil their biggest dreams. Recognising the challenges of staying focused and motivated, we’re creating a platform where users can set goals, find inspiration, and receive support. One key feature invites users to list their dreams for the year ahead, with rotating example dreams as placeholder text.
As you can imagine, this type of feature requires an array of exampleDreams
to cycle through. When I started to create the array, after a tiny bit of code, Copilot chimed in and started suggesting it’s own ideas!
It was so satisfying hitting tab, enter, tab, enter and thinking about how long it would have taken me to get that point without AI. I’m certainly no copywriter, but for me, this feature went from “I’ll just add some placeholder content” to “this is actually ready to go live” in zero time.
Seamless StackOverflow
Something else I never knew I needed was the ability to magically conjure up one-liners.
In a technical sense, the Dreams feature is a sortable array, but it also enables users to pull out individual dreams to focus on. Typically, in this scenario, I would Google, “remove specific item from array JavaScript.” I know what some of you are thinking — “Surely you just <insert obvious solution>” — but the truth is, despite over a decade of JavaScript experience, I have the memory of a goldfish! That part of my brain is permanently outsourced to Google and StackOverflow.
That is, until now. Instead of Googling, I just wrote a comment — // Get from dreams where focused is true
— and hey presto, Copilot just gave me the answer on the next line!
No leaving my IDE to search the internet. No scrolling through StackOverflow in search of that validating green tick. Just an instant, in-context solution — wonderful.
TypeScript Without the Tears
This is a controversial one for me, because I once wrote some stuff about how I don’t like TypeScript, and it ruffled a few feathers! For any of those reading this — you might be interested to know that I’ve done a complete 180! It’s true — it took me a while but I’m now at the stage where I can’t live without it (apologetic, grovelling article coming soon!).
That being said, it’s not super fun trawling through the TypeScript docs to understand why that red squiggly line keeps showing up. Again, Copilot saves a ton of time here. Clicking the “fix using Copilot” button means I can get a solution and an explanation instantly in my IDE.
Final Thoughts
I’ve only scratched the surface of what’s possible and I can confidently say, my dev experience is miles better than it was before. Don’t make the mistake of writing this stuff off as a fad — tools like Copilot are a game-changer and will massively increase your productivity. If you’re working on something right now, I recommend just going ahead and installing it — you’ll soon wonder how you ever worked without it.
